text.content:
The Seven-Cut Cairn — Location Brief

Surface threshold for the Seventh Stair. Southern Cairn Hills, off the abandoned Tarnsmere Spur, northeast of Greyhawk City. Phase 7 ends here. Phase 8 begins here.

Surface features

  • Ruined tollhouse. Two-room stone structure, no roof, the doorway lintel still standing. The interior is choked with rubble, but a stepped iron-bound door at the back of the inner room is visible, sealed shut with what appears to be old wax and rusted bands.
  • Collapsed survey station. Twenty yards downslope from the tollhouse: a stone foundation with the timber and slate that once made the walls now grown over with grass. A surveyor's iron stake remains driven into the corner of the foundation; the chain attached to it ends in nothing.
  • Half-buried stone marker. A waist-high cut-stone marker on the path between the tollhouse and the cairn, with the official Aerdy mining-road sign once stamped on its face — mostly weathered now to ghost lines.
  • The old mining spur road. The track that brought the party here. Ends here. Does not continue past the cairn.
  • Shallow quarry scars. On the hillside above the tollhouse, the old workings: half a dozen cut faces in the limestone, no shafts open, all of them quiet.
  • The cairn itself. Roughly chest-high, oval-footprint, built of locally quarried fieldstone and bound with old mortar. On its east face, cut into the largest single block, the route mark: seven descending cuts beneath a black hill symbol.
  • The blackened hill symbol. Not topographic. A surveyor's convention for a sealed entrance, with charcoal-blackening still visible despite weathering. The seventh cut is the sharpest of the seven, like it was made last and made deliberately.
  • Dry well or sealed stairhouse. Behind the ruined tollhouse, hidden by the foundation collapse, an opening that looks like a dry well from above but is in fact the top of a sealed stairwell. Phase 8 begins by opening this.
  • Signs of recent route-cleanser activity. If the Route-Cleansing Clock has reached 5, the seventh cut on the cairn has been scraped almost flat. If it has reached 6, the stairwell entrance is re-stoned and guarded.

Mood

  • Wind in the grass — constant, low, not loud.
  • The Greyhawk bells do not reach this far. The silence is not silence; it is administered.
  • The stone is too old for the current papers. The cuts on the cairn are not in any chart you have ever seen, and yet they are clearly the work of practiced hands.
  • Old cart ruts vanish into grass two hundred yards before the tollhouse.
  • Dusk light catches the cairn's seventh cut and turns the charcoal blackening to a faint glow that is not magical, only unsettling.
  • Crows. A few of them, far above, riding wind.

Tactical note for Phase 8

The cairn is not the entrance. The entrance is the sealed stairwell behind the ruined tollhouse. The cairn is the last surface marker. Whoever approaches the stairwell will be reading the cairn first — reading the permission. Phase 8 will deal with how to open the stairwell and what waits at the top of the descent.

Tactical note for Phase 7

Phase 7 ends with the discovery of this site. The party does not enter the stairwell in Phase 7. They may examine the cairn closely, take rubbings, study the stairwell from outside, photograph it (so to speak), and write it down. They do not descend. That is Phase 8.

Final Scene — Discovery

Late on the second day of field reconnaissance, as the sun is setting behind the western hills, the party crests a low rise and sees the site below them.

Read aloud — arrival at the cairn

The mining spur ends here. Two hundred yards of grass has grown up since anyone walked this road regularly; the cart ruts have vanished by degrees and then altogether. The road, which was a road this morning, is now a path; the path, which was a path an hour ago, is now a memory.

Below you, in a shallow fold of the hills, the ground levels out. There is a ruined tollhouse, its roof long fallen in, only the doorway-lintel still standing. Twenty yards beyond it, the stone foundation of what was once a survey station, the slate and timber long since grown over with grass. And beyond that, on a small rise where the path used to climb, a cairn.

The cairn is roughly chest-high. The stones are local; the mortar is old. The wind is in the grass. There are crows somewhere high above you, riding the air.

On the east face of the cairn, cut into the largest single block, in the old soot of a dozen winters: seven descending lines, beneath a hill that is not a hill.

The seventh cut is the sharpest of the seven. The blackening on the hill-symbol holds the last of the sunlight.

There are no Greyhawk bells. There has not been a Greyhawk bell since you turned onto the mining spur this morning.

The silence is not silence. It is administered.

Mechanical checks at the site

CheckDCReveals
Investigation (cairn)12The seventh cut was made last — later than the other six, by years if not decades.
History or Mining lore13Confirms Vetch's reading: the “hill” is a sealed-entrance convention. The cuts are a permission count.
Survival (signs of recent passage)13Three sets of boot prints in the last week. Two were merely walking the cairn; one came back and again, on a different night, with tools.
Perception (the tollhouse interior)13The rear inner wall has a stepped iron-bound door, sealed with wax and rusted bands. This is the stairwell entrance.
Arcana (residual magic on the cairn)14A faint, ancient, non-elemental enchantment lingers on the seven cuts. The party cannot read it. It feels like a contract.
Investigation (the cairn's wax recipe)14A wax fragment in a crevice of the cairn matches Aldea's unknown recipe-family. This is the place.

If the Route-Cleansing Clock is high

  • Segment 5: The seventh cut on the cairn has been scraped almost flat. The party can still confirm the site through wax-matching, the sealed stairwell, and Vetch's sketched map. Investigation DC 14 reveals the scraping is recent — within the last 48 hours.
  • Segment 6: The party arrives to find the ruined tollhouse occupied. Two route-cleansers and one hired tough are present, working to re-stone the stairwell entrance. The party can engage immediately, withdraw and observe, or attempt a peaceful capture. This combat is optional but plausible.

If a captured NPC is with the party

If the party brought Haskur, Kethren, or Korre to the site under guard, each reacts characteristically:

  • Haskur: shows no recognition. He has never been here. He says, quietly, “I was told only where to stand at the handoff. This is past where I was told.”
  • Kethren: looks at the seventh cut for a long time. Says nothing. Insight DC 14 reveals he is afraid. The fear is not surface-fear. It is the older kind.
  • Korre: looks at the cairn and shakes her head once. “This was a route-account on paper,” she says. “I never asked what the account was for. I'm sorry I never asked.”

Ending Read-Aloud

Read after the party has examined the cairn, identified the sealed stairwell, and made their camp at the site or above it. Pause for breath between the second-to-last line and the last.

It is full dark by the time you finish working the cairn. You have taken rubbings, you have copied the seventh cut three times in three different inks, you have stepped off the distance between the cairn and the ruined tollhouse, you have examined the stairwell's iron-bound door from every angle. The wax on its bands is the same recipe-family Aldea named. The stone above the lintel has a single small scratch you almost missed: seven descending lines, this time without the hill.

You make camp upslope from the tollhouse. There is no fire; the wind is too constant. You eat cold. You drink water you carried. You set a watch.

Above you, the Cairn Hills wind moves through the grass like a finger drawn across paper. Below you, the ruined tollhouse is a dark shape against a darker shape. Greyhawk City is twenty miles south and full of bells you will not hear tonight.

Someone — one of you, or the prisoner, or only the wind — speaks the phrase the cistern gave you, three nights ago and a hundred miles away:

“No sail. No road.”

The road ends at the cairn. The stair begins where the maps learned to lie.

Caelith's final courier note

If Caelith is still writing — that is, if Closure was held over or defeated, or if his last courier slipped past the Phase 5 leak — a folded paper arrives at the party's camp by indifferent rider before midnight. The note is unsigned. The hand is unmistakable.

You are no longer following a man. You are following the permissions around him.

Roads are watched from the sides. Descents are watched from below.

Whatever is at the foot of that stair, it was already there when the surface forgot how to read the marker.

Be ready. Be slow. Send word back when you can.

Final GM note

Phase 7 ends here. The party has the threshold. They have not entered it. The descent is for Phase 8.

Outcome Packages

Six end-state packages. Each carries forward into Phase 8 in a distinct way.

Outcome A — Full triangulation, low clock

Party reached Seven-Cut Cairn with all 5 evidence categories before route-cleansers finished. The Route-Cleansing Clock is at 3 or below. Phase 8 begins with the threshold intact but sealed. Standard Phase 8 setup.

Outcome B — Full triangulation, high clock

Party reached Seven-Cut Cairn with all 5 categories after significant enemy activity. The Route-Cleansing Clock is at 4–5. Waymark partially defaced. Stairwell entrance trapped or partially re-sealed. Phase 8 begins under pressure; the party will need to dismantle defenses before opening the threshold.

Outcome C — Partial triangulation

Party reached the right region (3 categories) and located the cairn through a short field-search sequence. Phase 8 begins with a short field-search scene at the start — the party knows the region but must locate the exact tollhouse. Enemy has time to prepare.

Outcome D — False stair first

Party was delayed by Node 6 (the False Stair). They recovered proof of misdirection but lost a day. Phase 8 begins with the enemy one step ahead: route-cleansers reach the cairn before the party and partially deface it. The stairwell entrance is intact but the surface site is being actively scrubbed when the party arrives.

Outcome E — Captured NPC helps

A prisoner or turned handler (Haskur, Kethren, Korre, Doras, or a turned route-cleanser) provides one key clue that accelerates triangulation. Party reaches the site faster. The prisoner becomes a liability or bargaining chip in Phase 8 setup: they know the party intends to descend, and they have an opinion about it.

Outcome F — Captured NPC escapes

A previously captured NPC escapes during Phase 7. The Route-Cleansing Clock advances by 1. The party can still reach the site through documents, but the conspiracy now has fresh intelligence about what the party knows. Phase 8 begins with the conspiracy adjusting defenses in real time.

Carryover to Phase 8

In all cases, the party carries to Phase 8:

  • The Ashen Itinerary (intact or fragmentary), Iren's analysis, Aldea's material reply.
  • Knowledge of the seven cuts under a black hill (the marker symbol).
  • Vetch's sketched map.
  • Whatever captured NPC(s) survived, plus their information.
  • Caelith's courier addendum.
  • The route mark sketch and a wax flake from the cairn site.

Phase 8 setup hooks

  • The sealed stairwell. Iron-bound door, old wax, rusted bands. Will not open to force without ritual or key.
  • The cairn's contract enchantment. A faint magical residue that suggests the threshold requires permission, not strength.
  • The wax recipe-family. Aldea has already seen it. So has Iren. So has the small black box of seal-wax samples in Iren's locked paper press.
  • The recall threat. If Kethren was captured, his fear of recall becomes more pressing as the party approaches the threshold.

Transition to Phase 8

Phase 8 working titles

Either of the following, whichever lands better at the table:

  • “The Stair Beneath Tarnsmere”
  • “The Threshold Below the Tollhouse”

Phase 8 purpose

  • Explore the ruined tollhouse and sealed stairwell.
  • Open or bypass the threshold.
  • Face the first true guardians of the deep route.
  • Recover proof that Haskur, Kethren, and the route passed through this point.
  • End with the party descending beyond the last surface marker.

Phase 9 forecast (for GM planning only)

Working title: “Below the Clean Paper.”

The first true below-ground conspiracy phase. Surface ledgers give way to deep logistics. The party begins to see who benefits from Blooming Rot — but not yet by name. Phase 9 may name the Underdark in player-facing text. Phase 7 and Phase 8 may not.

Phase 7 hard constraints (reminders)

  • Do not reveal the full conspiracy.
  • Do not name Eclavdra, House Eilserv, Vault.
  • Do not use the word Underdark in player-facing text.
  • Do not confirm the “E.” writer is non-human.
  • Do not make Haskur, Kethren, or Korre the mastermind.
  • Do not make Greyhawk City or the Guild broadly corrupt.
  • Do not require a single clue or single NPC for success.
  • Do not turn this into a large dungeon crawl. That is Phase 8.

The pivot

Phase 7 has been about turning a phrase into a place. Phase 8 will be about turning a place into a passage. The party came to Greyhawk City chasing a man and found a route-marker. Now they have walked to the route-marker and found a door. The door is the next phase.

Suggested closing GM line at the table

“You have the threshold. You have not opened it. When you are ready — when you have rested, and gathered, and brought what you need — we will pick up at the iron-bound door behind the ruined tollhouse, with the wax intact and the seven cuts above you on the cairn.”

“That is Phase 8.”
